structData.title: DSP vs GAM impression discrepancy — causes and acceptable thresholds
structData.content:
A reporting discrepancy is when the impression count in your DSP differs from the count in Google Ad Manager (GAM) or another third-party ad server for the same campaign. Some discrepancy is normal and expected. Large discrepancies indicate a configuration or tracking problem.

HOW TO DIAGNOSE
Call getDSPImpressionReport and getGAMReport for the same date range and compare impressions.
Call getDiscrepancyLog to see historical discrepancy percentages.

Calculate discrepancy: (DSP_impressions - GAM_impressions) / DSP_impressions × 100

ACCEPTABLE THRESHOLDS
  Below 10%: Normal — within industry standard. No action needed.
  10%–20%:   Elevated — investigate measurement methodology differences.
  Above 20%:  High — likely a tracking configuration error. Escalate.

WHY DISCREPANCIES OCCUR (NORMAL CAUSES)
1. Measurement timing: DSP counts an impression when the bid wins. GAM counts when the ad is rendered in the browser. Users who close the page before render are counted by DSP but not GAM.
2. Ad blockers: Some users have ad blockers that block the GAM tag but not the DSP bid. DSP counts the win, GAM never fires.
3. Latency filtering: GAM has a viewability threshold — ads that load but are not in the viewport may not be counted. DSP counts all served ads.
4. IVT filtering: GAM filters out identified bot traffic post-auction. DSP may count these before filtering. Resulting in DSP showing higher numbers.
5. Time zone differences: If DSP reports in UTC and GAM reports in advertiser local time, day-boundary impressions appear in different days.

HIGH DISCREPANCY CAUSES (NEED FIXING)
1. Wrong line item mapped: The GAM line item ID in your report does not correspond to the same campaign as your DSP campaign ID. Verify mapping.
2. GAM tag not firing: The GAM tag is missing from some placements. Check that every placement has the tag implemented.
3. Double counting: The same placement has both a DSP tag and a direct GAM tag — one impression gets counted twice.
4. Date range mismatch: DSP date range and GAM date range in your query are not aligned.

RECONCILIATION PROCESS
For discrepancies above 20%, raise a formal reconciliation request with your SSP/publisher. Provide getDSPImpressionReport and getGAMReport outputs. Industry standard is for the lower of the two counts to be used for billing. Most contracts specify the third-party ad server (GAM) as the measurement of record for billing disputes.
